title: dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta
intro: Dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta, commonly known as sticky fleabane, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties and is known to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which have been reported to provide various health benefits. The health benefits of sticky fleabane include its potential to treat wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders. The bioactive constituents of Dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta include sesquiterpene lactones, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic actions, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
benefits: Dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta has many benefits, such as its ability to heal wounds due to its antibacterial properties, which can aid in the treatment of cuts and scrapes, as well as reduce the risk of infection. This plant is also known to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in alleviating symptoms of conditions like arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory diseases. Additionally, the plant's antioxidant properties can help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases, such as cancer and heart disease. By incorporating Dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta into one's life, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and enhanced quality of life.
actions: Dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, heal wounds, and protect against cell damage. intro_study: According to "Planta medica", dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta has gastroprotective effects, reducing gastric lesions and increasing mucus production.
constituents: Dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenoids, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These compounds, including kaempferol, quercetin, and luteolin, have been shown to improve people's daily lives by reducing the risk of chronic diseases, such as heart disease and certain types of cancer. Additionally, the plant's sesquiterpenoids, including carotenoids and sesquiterpene lactones, have been found to have anti-cancer properties, and its flavonoids have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving blood lipid profiles. By utilizing the medicinal constituents of Dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta, people can potentially lead healthier, happier lives, with reduced risks of chronic diseases and improved overall well-being.
parts: Dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, and roots, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine. The leaves of Dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta are rich in flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, and have been used to treat various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and inflammation. The flowers of the plant are rich in essential oils, including geraniol, limonene, and beta-pinene, and have been used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions. The roots of Dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta contain sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, and have been used to treat conditions such as fever and rheumatism.
side_effects: Dittrichia viscosa subsp. revoluta can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Some people may experience respiratory issues, including asthma-like symptoms and bronchospasm, when exposed to the plant's volatile oils. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can also lead to skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.
